track.title: Jua Kali Daily Income Plan
track.description: A daily-cash route for informal workers and small operators separating stock, household needs, emergency money, and reinvestment.

course.title: Jua Kali Daily Income Plan: Core route
course.description: Turn irregular daily income into a simple route for protection, learning, and careful growth.

lessons.title: Daily income needs daily buckets
lessons.summary: Daily earners need a simple split that protects stock, rent, food, transport, and emergency money.
lessons.introduction: Jua kali and informal workers may earn today, spend today, and still need to prepare for tomorrow. A daily bucket system is not about perfection; it is a practical way to stop stock money, household money, and learning money from fighting each other.
sections: Stock or tool money should be replaced before optional spending because it keeps income alive.
Household needs can be recorded as weekly targets even when income arrives daily.
A small emergency reserve can reduce the need to borrow at high cost when tools break or work slows.

scenario.title: Repair fund for a fundi
scenario.text: A fundi earns KES 1,800 in a day and separates transport, food, tool repair reserve, stock replacement, and a small learning amount.

lessons.mistake: Treating all cash in hand as profit before replacing stock, tools, or transport money.
lessons.action: Write a four-bucket split for one day: work cost, home cost, emergency reserve, and learning/reinvestment.
lessons.takeaway: Daily money needs a daily job description.
lessons.source_note: Source note: This is practical education, not financial advice or product recommendation.

lessons.title: Business reinvestment vs personal savings
lessons.summary: Business money and personal money can both matter, but mixing them hides the true picture.
lessons.introduction: A small business can look profitable while quietly losing money if stock replacement, rent, transport, airtime, packaging, repairs, and personal withdrawals are mixed together. The learner should separate business reinvestment from personal savings before considering external products.
sections: Business reinvestment keeps income generation working: stock, tools, display, delivery, or repairs.
Personal savings protects the person and household: emergency fund, fees, rent, and planned goals.
If the business needs money next week, it should not be placed in a route that cannot be accessed quickly.

scenario.title: Salon supplies and home rent
scenario.text: A salon operator records daily sales, replaces supplies, sets aside rent target, and avoids using supplier money for a long-lockup route.

lessons.mistake: Calling daily sales profit without subtracting stock, rent, labour, and replacement costs.
lessons.action: For one day, record sales, direct costs, business reserve, personal reserve, and what remains.
lessons.takeaway: Clear separation shows whether growth is real.
lessons.source_note: Source note: This lesson teaches record habits; verify product terms before using any provider.

lessons.title: Choosing liquidity before return
lessons.summary: Use a simulator to compare short-term access needs before deciding where learning money sits.
lessons.introduction: For daily earners, liquidity can be more important than the headline return. Money needed for stock, rent, repairs, or emergency transport should be easy to access before a user considers longer-term routes.
sections: Short-term money should be labelled by date needed.
An MMF simulator can teach yield and withdrawal assumptions, but it does not execute investment.
A SACCO route may support discipline and borrowing access, but deposits, share capital, and rules must be understood.

scenario.title: Stock money due Friday
scenario.text: A trader has KES 12,000 for stock due Friday and KES 2,000 for learning. They test only the learning amount in a simulator.

lessons.mistake: Putting supplier money into a route because the return sounds attractive.
lessons.action: Run a simulator using only money that is not needed for immediate business operations.
lessons.takeaway: Liquidity protects the hustle before growth plans begin.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Provider terms, withdrawal timelines, and fees must be checked before committing money.

lessons.title: Jua kali journal at closing time
lessons.summary: A short closing-time journal can capture sales, costs, and tomorrow's money needs.
lessons.introduction: A daily journal entry does not need accounting language. It can simply record what came in, what must go back into work, what supports the household, and what question needs learning before money moves.
sections: Use rounded or range amount mode if exact numbers feel too sensitive.
Write one sentence about what went well and one risk for tomorrow.
A weekly review can show whether emergency reserve and business reserve are improving.

scenario.title: Evening note
scenario.text: A boda spare-parts seller records sales range, stock replacement, home support, and a question about SACCO rules.

lessons.mistake: Depending on memory for daily money decisions in a busy business.
lessons.action: Create one journal entry after work: income range, business reserve, personal reserve, and tomorrow's first expense.
lessons.takeaway: Small daily records create better weekly decisions.
lessons.source_note: Source note: This is an educational habit prompt, not tax, accounting, or investment advice.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should be separated from daily sales?
quiz_questions.explanation: Mixing them hides whether the business is truly growing.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should happen to stock money needed soon?
options: Send it to an unknown agent
Each shilling gets a clear job
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Each shilling gets a clear job
quiz_questions.explanation: Buckets reduce confusion between work, home, emergency, and learning money.

flashcards.back: Money needed to replace goods, materials, or tools used to earn income.
flashcards.example: Charcoal, salon supplies, spare parts, fabric, or food stock.

flashcards.back: Putting money back into the work that produces income.
flashcards.example: Repairing tools or buying faster-moving stock.

flashcards.back: Money protected for household or personal needs.
flashcards.example: Rent, food, transport, school fees, or emergency cash.

flashcards.back: It shows whether money can be placed in a slower route.
A short note at the end of the day about cash, costs, and tomorrow.
flashcards.example: Sales range, stock reserve, home support, next expense.

resources.title: Jua kali daily money checklist
resources.body: A daily checklist for informal workers and small operators: separate work costs, home costs, emergency reserve, and learning money before using any investment route.
items: Record today's cash in as exact, rounded, range, or hidden.
Replace stock, tools, transport, or supplier money first.
Set aside household and emergency needs.
Use only non-urgent learning money in simulations.
